features.description: Investigate original issue #117 and automation failure context
features.details: Examine issue #117 to understand what type of processing was being attempted when the failure occurred. Review any logs or stack traces that show the full error context and identify the specific code path that triggered the AttributeError.

features.description: Locate the failing code accessing 'head' attribute
features.details: Search the automation codebase for all instances where '.head' is accessed on a PullRequest object. Identify the exact file(s) and line numbers where the AttributeError originates.

Review the GitHub API library documentation (likely PyGithub or similar) to identify the correct way to access the head/ref information from a PullRequest object. This may be 'head_ref', 'head.sha', or require a different access pattern.
Update the code to use the correct property/method to access PullRequest head information. Ensure the fix handles cases where the attribute may be None or unavailable.
Add try-except blocks and proper error handling around PullRequest attribute access to provide more informative error messages and prevent similar failures in the future.

features.description: Add unit tests for PullRequest attribute access
features.details: Create unit tests that mock PullRequest objects and verify the correct access patterns. Include tests for edge cases like missing attributes or None values.

features.description: Audit codebase for similar attribute access issues
features.details: Search the entire codebase for similar patterns where GitHub API objects may have incorrect attribute access. Look for other PullRequest, Issue, or Repository attribute accesses that may be using outdated or incorrect property names.
After implementing the fix, manually trigger or simulate the processing of issue #117 to verify the automation now works correctly. Close issue #121 once confirmed working.

overall_approach: Investigate the automation codebase to locate where the 'head' attribute is being accessed on a PullRequest object, determine the correct API/property to use (likely 'head_ref' or accessing via the GitHub API differently), implement the fix, and add regression tests.
potential_risks: The fix may require changes to multiple files if the incorrect attribute access is used in multiple places
GitHub API version or library differences may affect the correct property name
Other similar attribute access issues may exist elsewhere in the codebase
The original issue #117 may have specific requirements that need separate handling after the fix
